summary:
• Hormuz Strait transit disruptions are creating severe supply route vulnerabilities for Western Hemisphere refiners and traders, with India facing oil crisis impacts and Taiwan's power grid threatened by constrained shipments.

• Mexico's Pemex leadership transition amid ongoing production challenges compounds Western Hemisphere supply concerns, particularly as Canadian pipeline expansion plans face political reconsideration and regulatory uncertainty.

• Iran geopolitical tensions are escalating crude volatility with Trump administration pressure driving Brent toward 6% weekly gains, while coordinated Chinese tanker transits signal potential for further market-moving shifts in regional power dynamics.
